2606.22858.title: The Unseen Hand: Manipulating Model Fairness and SHAP with Targeted Identity Re-Association Attacks
2606.22858.paper_summary: As machine learning models grow more influential and opaque, algorithmic fairness and explainability are critical for ensuring accountability. However, we demonstrate that these auditing mechanisms are themselves vulnerable to subtle manipulation, camouflaging the influence of protected features. While prior work on data-agnostic attacks has exposed this vulnerability, they leave behind detectable artifacts that compromise their stealth. We introduce Targeted Identity Re-Association (TIRA) attacks, a novel family of attacks that iteratively and probabilistically manipulate a model's outputs without requiring access to the model's internals or feature representations. We formalize two algorithms: Probabilistic Micro-Shuffling (PMiS), which applies localized adjacent swaps, and Probabilistic Rank-Shift Micro-Perturbation (PRSMP), which introduces small, randomized rank shifts. We empirically demonstrate that TIRA attacks are highly effective at pushing fairness metrics towards ideal values. Crucially, TIRA attacks successfully confound SHAP-based explanations, leaving effectively zero residual attribution for protected features, a major improvement over prior work.
2606.22858.paper_summary_zh: 隨著機器學習模型變得越來越有影響力且不透明，算法公平性和可解釋性對於確保問責制至關重要。<lb/>然而，我們證明這些審計機制本身易受到微妙操控的影響，掩蓋了受保護特徵的影響。<lb/>雖然先前關於數據無關攻擊的研究揭示了這種脆弱性，但它們留下了可檢測的痕跡，妨礙了其隱蔽性。<lb/>我們引入了目標身份重新關聯（TIRA）攻擊，這是一種新型攻擊家族，它以迭代和概率的方式操控模型的輸出，而無需訪問模型的內部或特徵表示。<lb/>我們形式化了兩種算法：概率微洗牌（PMiS），它應用局部相鄰交換，以及概率排名偏移微擾（PRSMP），它引入小的隨機排名偏移。<lb/>我們實證證明TIRA攻擊在將公平性指標推向理想值方面非常有效。<lb/>至關重要的是，TIRA攻擊成功混淆基於SHAP的解釋，對受保護特徵留下幾乎為零的殘餘歸因，這是對先前工作的重大改進。
2606.22858.author: Sannaan Khan et.al.
2606.22858.authors: Sannaan Khan, Muhammad U. S. Khan
